confA.description: Conference room A - orientation 1 (first test setup)
confA.layout: RX and TX both along the short bottom wall, 88 in apart. Door on the right wall, mid-room.
confA.tx_position: bottom wall, 88 in right of RX (door side)

confA_o2.layout: RX and TX along the LEFT long wall, 106 in apart; link oriented down the room's length. Camera between them, closer to the RX side. Door on right wall, mid-room.
confA_o2.note: Same room as confA but the link is rotated ~90 deg (short-wall 88in -> long-wall 106in). Used to test whether the presence model generalizes beyond one geometry's static fingerprint.

confA_o3.layout: RX and TX along the TOP short wall (the 223 in wall), 66 in apart; opposite short wall from orientation 1. Camera between them. Door on right wall, mid-room.
confA_o3.note: Third distinct link geometry. Enables train-on-2-orientations / test-on-the-held-out-3rd (leave-one-room-out) = the definitive portability test.

home_L.description: Home room (new, 2026-06-26) - L-shaped, floor-markable, more open space
home_L.grid: origin (0,0) = bottom-left corner; x along 150in bottom wall {0,30,60,90,120,150} cols A-F; y up {0,30,60,90,120} rows 1-5; 30in (2.5ft) spacing
home_L.note: First non-office environment. Enough clear floor to TAPE A COORDINATE GRID -> position/localization ground truth (homography) and a controlled coverage grid become possible. Origin = bottom-left corner (near the 'X' start); x-axis along the 150in bottom wall, y-axis up the walls; L-notch at top-left. Use for cross-environment generalization + position work; also vary node ORIENTATION here.
